zh.lifeSummary: Margaret Burghersh是Sir John de Burghersh, Knt和Ismania Lady Baroness Kerdeston deHanham的女兒，出生在英國英格蘭賀特福爾。她於大約1391年與Sir John de Grenville在英國可瓦爾結婚。她去世於大約1421年在英國可瓦爾藍黑爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Margaret Burghersh是Sir John de Burghersh, Knt和Ismania Lady Baroness Kerdeston deHanham的女儿，出生在英国英格兰賀特福爾。她于大约1391年与Sir John de Grenville在英国可瓦爾结婚。她去世于大约1421年在英国可瓦爾藍黑爾。

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Margaret Burghersh was born in Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom as the daughter of Sir John de Burghersh, Knt and Ismania Lady Baroness Kerdeston deHanham. She married Sir John de Grenville about 1391, in Cornwall, England. She died about 1421, in Lanherne, Cornwall, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret Burghersh est née à Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fille de Sir John de Burghersh, Knt et Ismania Lady Baroness Kerdeston deHanham. Elle a épousé Sir John de Grenville environ 1391, à Cornouailles, Angleterre. Elle est décédée environ 1421, à Lanherne, Cornouailles, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Margaret Burghersh wurde in Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom, als Tochter von Sir John de Burghersh, Knt und Ismania Lady Baroness Kerdeston deHanham geboren. Sie hat um 1391 in Cornwall, England Sir John de Grenville geheiratet. Sie starb um 1421 in Lanherne, Cornwall, England.

it.lifeSummary: Margaret Burghersh è nata il nel Hertfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito come figlia di Sir John de Burghersh, Knt e Ismania Lady Baroness Kerdeston deHanham. Lei sposato Sir John de Grenville circa 1391, nel Cornwall, England. Morì circa 1421, nel Lanherne, Cornwall, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret BurghershはイギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアでSir John de Burghersh, KntとIsmania Lady Baroness Kerdeston deHanhamの娘として生まれた。約1391年にイギリスコルンワルでSir John de Grenvilleと結婚した。彼女は約1421年にイギリスコルンワルランハーンで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Burghersh(은)는 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르에서 Sir John de Burghersh, Knt(와)과 Ismania Lady Baroness Kerdeston deHanham의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1391년 영국 코른월에서 Sir John de Grenville(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 대략 1421년 영국 코른월 랜헤른에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Margaret Burghersh nasceu em Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom filha de Sir John de Burghersh, Knt e Ismania Lady Baroness Kerdeston deHanham. Ela casou-se com Sir John de Grenville aproximadamente 1391, em Cornwall, Inglaterra. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1421, em Lanherne, Cornwall, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret Burghersh родилась в местности хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. Её родителей звали Sir John de Burghersh, Knt и Ismania Lady Baroness Kerdeston deHanham. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John de Grenville около 1391 г. в местности корнвалл, Англия. Она умерла около 1421 г. в местности ланирн, корнвалл, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Margaret Burghersh nació en Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido como hija de Sir John de Burghersh, Knt y Ismania Lady Baroness Kerdeston deHanham. Se casó con Sir John de Grenville aproximadamente 1391, en Cornwall, Inglaterra. Murió aproximadamente 1421, en Lanherne, Cornwall, Inglaterra.
